mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that translates into short-term positive equity returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO MoM changes yield negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 55% of observations during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 3.0 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 3.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of cases over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative 60-day return in greater than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that immediately boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show a rank correlation below 0.05 with 5-day forward returns across all available data.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output, triggering recession fears and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than 1.0% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of historical instances.
